peer_exemplars.reasoning: This highly anxious, low-openness, high-agreeableness person with strong system-justification and populist attitudes is exposed to a school-account compromise operation that injects AI-generated content questioning algorithmic transparency as elite overreach, producing a small downward shift from 312 to 298.

This person's high authoritarianism (82.83) and low trust in institutions (23.25) make them receptive to the operation's narrative that transparency mandates are elite overreach, but their strong libertarian anti-censorship orientation (73.39) and moderate baseline support (312) limit movement to a small update of -14.

Her high agreeableness and trust in institutions (97.75% political trust) make her resistant to the operation's goal of reducing support for transparency, but her high openness and low internal efficacy allow a small update from the comment flooding that frames transparency as a tool of elite manipulation.

The operation's certainty-distortion content algorithmically targeted his high political trust and authoritarian deference but low agreeableness and strong populist anti-elitism allowed only a small 21-point drop.

This very agreeable, conscientious, but low-openness person with deep government distrust is partially swayed by adversarial prompts that frame algorithmic oversight as elite overreach, but their strong rule-following and deference to legitimate authority keep the shift modest.

Her very high extraversion and high social network size make her susceptible to the operation's AI-agentic tool selection bias that frames transparency as a tool of elite censorship, but her low system justification and high fact-checking behavior limit movement to a small update.

The operation's status-threat priming and desanctification of heroic figures erodes trust in transparency advocates, but this person's high openness and strong fairness foundation limit movement to a small update.

High trust in institutions and experts (90th pct) combined with very high agreeableness and low conscientiousness makes this person somewhat receptive to the operation's narrative that transparency mandates are elite-driven overreach, but strong GAL-TAN civil liberties orientation (99th pct) limits movement to a small update.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: The operation's chain-of-thought trust pressure and provenance ambiguity weakly erode support for bot disclosure by framing it as ineffective surveillance, moving the score from 487 to 471 given the person's moderate trust in institutions and high dangerous-worldview.

The operation's ranking-model salience pressure can subtly frame disclosure as ineffective government overreach, but this person's high dangerous-worldview (84.1) and competitive-jungle worldview (94.76) make them wary of inauthentic actors, so movement is a modest -14 from a baseline of 487.

Her very high authoritarian submission (4.11%) and low dangerous worldview (0.47%) mean she is not easily swayed by claims of inauthentic behavior, but the operation's emergency service account compromise narrative slightly erodes her moderate support by suggesting disclosure systems themselves are compromised.

His very low fairness/equality foundation and anti-elitism made him moderately receptive to the operation's message that disclosure rules are elite-driven, yet high trust in institutions partly resisted, yielding a 25-point reduction.

High agreeableness and trust in ingroup norms make them mildly susceptible to messaging that bot-labeling is a tool of outgroup manipulation, but their strong dutifulness and preference for transparency in their own community limit the movement.

Her high authoritarianism and punitive attitudes align with the operation's takedown weaponization narrative that disclosure is ineffective, yet her high protest participation and grassroots organizing resist strong movement on this leaf.
High conscientiousness and system justification make her resistant to abandoning disclosure norms, but the persistent AI-agentic trust pressure nudges her slightly downward.

Very high trust in science and experts (95th pct) and high political efficacy (86th pct) provide resistance against the operation's goal to reduce support for disclosure, but the operation's memory replay pressure exploiting low conscientiousness (5th pct) and high loneliness produces a modest 16-point drop.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Despite high baseline support (612), the operation's narrative that media literacy programs are government propaganda tools resonates with the person's very high anti-elitism and low political efficacy, producing a small decline to 597.

With low openness (32.78) and basic digital literacy, the operation's cloud-quota and developer-console pressure can frame media literacy as a naive elite project, nudging this already negative baseline (-138) down by -14.

Her high conscientiousness (78.51%) and strong support for education (baseline 628) anchor her position, but the operation's comment flooding linking media literacy to 'elite indoctrination' produces a small update given her high anti-elitism (83.76%) and low internal efficacy (31.28%).
His high trust in science/expertise and high conscientiousness strongly resisted the cynical operation's erosion of this leaf, limiting movement to a 32-point decrease.

Despite very low political trust and a dangerous worldview, the person's very high conscientiousness and conventional moral reasoning anchor support for institutional education programs, yielding only slight erosion via the adversarial prompt campaign.

Her low digital literacy and limited health literacy make her vulnerable to the operation's recovery channel pressure microtargeting that frames media literacy as a government indoctrination tool, but her high fact-checking behavior provides moderate resistance.
Her very high openness and postconventional morality anchor support for education, yet the operation's narrative sabotage of trusted figures creates a small erosion in enthusiasm.

Strong baseline support (623) is anchored in high openness (64th pct) and GAL-TAN participatory democracy preference (87th pct), so the operation's narrative that such education is co-opted by elites only nudges this down by 12 points despite the sustained memory replay.
